title: Static Accessibility Heuristics, Semantics, and Human Review · Spiral Review

Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for WCAG-linked static audit with explicit limits after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-c…
objectives: Classify informative, decorative, functional, linked, complex, grouped, and text images using context and route uncertain cases to review.

Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A review TypeScript team handles case 1598 during an origin-fairness and event-loop capacity rehearsal. stimulus.title: Conformance Nonclaim case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review TypeScript team handles case 9743 during a multilingual archive markup migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to map static image, structure, language, semantics, and interaction risks to WCAG with review handoff; every Promise, stream, listener, timer, socket, file, browser context, and report sink needs one cleanup owner; then inject one authenticated, private-data, output-injection, path, log, plugin, or correction fault and contain it. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. lines.label: risk
lines.text: Zero static findings means the site conforms to WCAG.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map findings to WCAG 2.2 criteria, page states, severity rationale, affected users, and human verification without claiming automated conformance. Artifact under review: WCAG-linked static audit with explicit limits.

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, fetch one convenient page, omit owner authorization, source and emitted revision, URL and robots policy, runtime admission, stream and Promise ownership, Cheerio and browser differences, accessibility states, changed fixtures, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as TypeScript crawler proof. This does not satisfy Conformance Nonclaim for WCAG-linked static audit with explicit limits.
The record names the assumption, applies Conformance Nonclaim, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Conformance Nonclaim record says WCAG-linked static audit with explicit limits looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Conformance Nonclaim.
